meta.title: REZICS: Encounter the stories you love
meta.description: Rezics is a place where stories, knowledge, reviews, communities, and people come back together through cross-platform, multilingual, openly editable, and structured community foundations.

sections.title: After an adventure ends, the story should not stop at the final page.
sections.body: People want to write reactions, find others, organize the world, ask about confusing details, read interpretations, and sometimes begin new creations. These actions all gather around the same story, yet they are often forced into different places.

sections.title: Catalogs, wikis, forums, and groups are valuable, but each carries only part of the experience.
sections.body: A catalog can list works, a wiki can preserve knowledge, a forum can host discussion, a group can gather people, and a rating site can hold reactions. When they stay separated, stories, knowledge, reviews, and communities cannot become a place that keeps growing.

sections.title: Rezics treats cross-platform, multilingual, and open collaboration as foundations from the start.
sections.body: Stories do not belong to one platform, and they should not be trapped in one language. Rezics connects sources, languages, and community perspectives through i18n-first design, open editing, structured data, and community consensus.

storySections.title: Give stories a public identity that crosses platforms, languages, and media.
storySections.body: Works, series, worlds, interactive fiction, games, and hybrid creations need identities that can be cited, translated, expanded, and discussed. This is where reviews, wiki, tags, realms, and zones begin to connect.

storySections.title: Let stories become places where people, knowledge, reactions, and communities gather.
storySections.body: When you want to discuss a novel, organize a world's chronology, or ask about a confusing passage, you should not have to leave the story and start over somewhere else. Rezics brings those actions back beside the story.

storySections.title: Classification is not a platform's one-way answer; it is consensus communities can build.
storySections.body: Global tags provide shared language, while realm votes let communities judge whether a tag truly fits a story from their own perspective. Classification can move across communities while keeping context.

storySections.title: Rezics respects every voice and believes open discussion needs structure.
storySections.body: We defend freedom of expression, but freedom should not mean information disappears into a feed. Forum-grade discussion, editable knowledge, traceable sources, and recognizable community context help voices be seen and understood.
